title: Three-Dimensional Two-Band Crossing Symbols
summary: This notation map fixes the symbols used in Lecture One for the local 2x2 crossing Hamiltonian, its Taylor expansion, and the resulting chirality sign.
assumptions: The local band problem has already been reduced to a two-band description.
scope: Reusable symbol map for the local crossing-to-Weyl derivation.

source_anchors.notes: The notation here is what the local linearization and chirality formula reuse downstream.

representation: Symbol table for the local crossing Hamiltonian and its Jacobian.

symbols.meaning: scalar shift of the local 2x2 Hamiltonian
Pauli-vector coefficients whose zero locates the crossing
Pauli matrices acting on the local two-band space
Jacobian of the Pauli-vector coefficients at the bad point

retrieval_hints: Use whenever a proof or regression answer needs to state Witten's local symbols explicitly.
